text:
Epilepsy Centre

Neurosciences Division

From: Dr Emily Turner (KING'S COLLEGE HOSPITAL NHS FOUNDATION TRUST)
Sent: 04 April 2023 10:30
To: epilepsy.centre@nhs.net
Cc: Dr Ravi Shah (KING'S COLLEGE HOSPITAL NHS FOUNDATION TRUST); Dr Hannah Lewis (KING'S COLLEGE HOSPITAL NHS FOUNDATION TRUST)
Subject: Easy-read information resources

Hi Emily

Patient update: Currently not driving by self-report.

I am writing to let you know that our service has produced new easy-read materials covering clinic pathways, appointment expectations, medication monitoring, and how to contact the team. These are designed to support shared decision-making and improve accessibility for patients and carers.

Access options:
- Printed copies available at reception on request
- PDF and large-print versions via our service email
- Audio versions available upon request

If you or your patients would like copies, please reply to this email indicating preferred format and language. We can also provide summaries for care coordinators and community teams.

With best wishes

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: I reviewed the letter for any mention of seizures or seizure frequency, including explicit numbers, ranges, or qualitative descriptions. The content focuses on easy-read information resources and includes a note that the patient is currently not driving, but there is no mention of seizures or their frequency. Since seizures are not referenced at all, per the rules, the correct label is 'no seizure frequency reference'.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter notifying availability of easy-read materials about epilepsy services

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
